meta.solver: openEMS (source-built, 2026-07-11), MUR x6 boundaries, Gaussian f0=2 GHz fc=1 GHz
meta.recipe: openEMS Simple_Patch_Antenna tutorial recipe: AddEdges2Grid(dirs='xy') thirds-rule metal-edge mesh on patch (metal_edge_res=mesh_res/2) and ground, plus explicit feed mesh lines at x=-6 mm / y=0 (load-bearing: without them the lumped port is an unused primitive and the run stays at zero energy)

geometry.realized_grid: 49x47x42 = 96726 cells, dt = 1.43245e-12 s (from the run log)

run.settling_note: stopped on EndCriteria=1e-4 with last printed energy -41.09 dB — clears the -40 dB ring-down bar; the recipe records a hard reason not to extend (NrTS past ~85k showed late-onset instability in this openEMS build)

meta.extraction: resonance: rfx harminv on the openEMS port voltage trace port_ut_1 (skip first 20%, band 1.0-3.5 GHz, filters Q>2 amp>1e-8, mode nearest the S11 dip); far field: openEMS NF2FF theta=0:2:180 deg, phi={0,90,180,270}, center (0,0,sub_thick/2)
meta.source_script: scripts/diagnostics/patch_tutorial_openems.py (tracked in this repo as the oracle provenance)
meta.source_result_json: scripts/diagnostics/cv05_investigation_results/patch_tutorial_openems_GOOD.json (local-only investigation dir, untracked; byte-identical sibling copy patch_tutorial_openems.json verified with diff)
meta.log_path: docs/research_notes/vessl_logs/patch_tutorial_openems_GOOD_369367246713.log (docs/research_notes is local-only/gitignored in this public repo; log lines quoted verbatim in the reproduce_gate detail below)

reproduce_gate.detail: SUBSTANCE: satisfied — the producing script IS the tutorial replication (the target geometry is the canonical Simple_Patch geometry; no geometry swap ever occurred) and the GOOD run reproduced the tutorial's expected known-good behaviour: clean broadside pattern, D=6.79 dBi vs the '~7 dBi' published anchor, f_res=2.4221 GHz vs the ~2.4 GHz tutorial design. Log prints verbatim: 'harminv-port-V f_res=2.4221 GHz Q=20.1 | S11 dip 2.4300 (-27.8 dB)' and 'directivity 6.79 dBi | BROADSIDE OK'. LETTER: the research/CLAUDE.md reproduce-gate asks the script itself to record the reproduced number + log path + delta list BEFORE any geometry change; that docstring artifact was missing at the time of the run and was added retroactively (2026-07-18, with the delta list vs upstream python/Tutorials/Simple_Patch_Antenna.py). The strict record-first ordering therefore cannot be retroactively satisfied, but no geometry swap occurred that the ordering exists to guard against.
reproduce_gate.consequence: no precise upstream-published number (f_res / S11 / D with an openEMS-project citation) is recorded anywhere in this repo — only the loose '~7 dBi broadside' anchor. 2.4221 GHz / 6.79 dBi are therefore OUR source-built-openEMS run's measured values, and this fixture is a committed-openEMS-reference ENVELOPE LOCK (the cv06b precedent, tests/fixtures/msl_notch_e4/), NOT a claim of matching openEMS's published tutorial result.
reproduce_gate.follow_up: tracked follow-up before any claim stronger than an envelope lock: a fresh external-lane re-verification with the literal-tutorial-first structure (run the upstream tutorial verbatim, record its number and log, then re-run our instrumented variant)

meta.claim: canonical-patch far-field reference for tests/test_patch_canonical_farfield_e4.py: the identical-geometry rfx lane (scripts/diagnostics/patch_tutorial_rfx.py) measured broadside D within 0.08 dB (6.71 vs 6.79 dBi, np=250 research frame) and a documented coarse-dx (dx=2 mm) resonance offset of -8.6% (2.2143 vs 2.4221 GHz) — an envelope regression lock, not an accuracy claim
